content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Carol Ann Butler 
Deputy city Clerk 
P.O. Box 3707 
November 13, 1989 
Sunnyvale, CA 
94086-3707 
Dear Ms. Butler: 
Re: 
Your Request For Advice 
Our File No. A-89-579 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you as a filing officer for the City of Sunnyvale concerning 
the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.~/ 
Your letter, dated October 2, 1989, correctly summarizes the 
advice I provided to you regarding your duties as a filing officer 
under the Political Reform Act, except as indicated below. 
As we 
discussed on October 24, 1989, you should fully review all 
campaign disclosure statements which were filed during the years 
1985-1988 by current elected city officeholders, their controlled 
committees, ballot measure committees and general purpose 
committees. 
Enclosed is a copy of the Handbook for city and 
County Filing Officers which outlines the duties of filing 
officers. 
contained in the Handbook is Regulation 18110, which 
identifies the specific items that filing officers must review on 
the campaign disclosure statements. (See Appendix B.) 
In addition, campaign statements filed by unsuccessful 
candidates during the past four years should receive a full review 
for the years 1987 and 1988, and a facial review for 1985 and 
1986. 
A facial review consists of reviewing statements for the 
following items: 
(1) The cover sheet includes the name and 
address of the filer, the period covered and the type of 
statement; (2) the summary page is completed, and the required 
schedules are attached; (3) all information is legible, and 
readable reproductions of the statement can be made; and (4) the 
verification is complete. 
~/ Government 
references are 
ss 
Section 18000, 
Code Sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.

California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Carol Ann Butler 
Deputy City Clerk 
P.O. Box 3707 
Sunnyvale, CA 
94086-3707 
Dear Ms. Butler: 
October 10, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-579 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on October 5, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
r 
, , 
, Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief Technical Assistance 
and Analysis Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660

." 


October 2, 1989 
Alice Hughes 
Fair Poli tical Practices Commission 
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, Ca. 
95804-0807 
Dear Alice: 
Ocr 5 
As we discussed on the telephone last week 1 would be very grateful if you could send 
me clarification regarding specifics on the FPPC Document check list. 
You indicated that the forms prior to 1985 should be logged and filed; forms from 1985-
89 logged and filed and filing officer is required to notify filer in writing if their name 
is deleted on form; forms filed from 1988-89 should be checked for any discrepancies 
as outlined in Filing Officer Manual, and that the filing officer is required to notify 
filer in writing of the specific discrepancy. 
As I understand only the forms filed for 
the past year need to be checked for discrepancies in order to notify filer of incomplete 
forms. 
Alice, hopefully this will held me to streamline the checklist procedure. 
I've logged 
and filed all FPPC forms from 1975 to current, however, I still need something in 
writing from your office which would clarify exactly what I am responsible for in the 
notification procedure. 
Thanks again for being so helpful during this "ordeal" - whiC:h it has certainly turned out 
to be. 
tier 
City Clerk 
ADDRESS ALL MAIL TO: P.O. BOX 3707 
SUNNYVALE, CALIFORNIA 94086-3707 
For deaf access, call TOD/TTY (408) 730-7501 
'89

sections.question: Clarification regarding specifics on the FPPC Document checklist, including which forms from which years should be logged, filed, and reviewed, and what notification procedures are required of the filing officer for incomplete or deficient forms.
sections.conclusion: Campaign disclosure statements filed during 1985-1988 by current elected officeholders and their committees should receive full review; campaign statements filed by unsuccessful candidates should receive full review for 1987-1988 and facial review for 1985-1986. A facial review consists of verifying the cover sheet, summary page completion, legibility, and verification completeness.
sections.question_synthetic: As a filing officer for the City of Sunnyvale, Ms. Butler seeks clarification on which campaign disclosure statements from which years require full review versus facial review, and what her notification obligations are regarding incomplete or deficient forms.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Filing officers must fully review statements from current elected officials for 1985-1988, but unsuccessful candidate statements require only facial review for earlier years. Facial review focuses on cover sheet accuracy, summary page completion, legibility, and verification.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple letters within single submission: incoming request (Oct 2, 1989), acknowledgment letter (Oct 10, 1989), and formal advice response (Nov 13, 1989). Extracted primary FPPC response (Nov 13 letter from Alice Hughes/Kathryn Donovan). No traditional QUESTION/CONCLUSION section headers, but content clearly delineates question and conclusion elements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Clarification regarding specifics on the FPPC Document checklist, including which forms from which years should be logged, filed, and reviewed, and what notification procedures are required of the filing officer for incomplete or deficient forms.

CONCLUSION: Campaign disclosure statements filed during 1985-1988 by current elected officeholders and their committees should receive full review; campaign statements filed by unsuccessful candidates should receive full review for 1987-1988 and facial review for 1985-1986. A facial review consists of verifying the cover sheet, summary page completion, legibility, and verification completeness.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirms telephone advice provided to the Deputy City Clerk of Sunnyvale regarding her duties as a filing officer to review campaign disclosure statements, specifying different review standards for current officials versus unsuccessful candidates based on the filing year.
